title: PrintSettingQuery Object
description: A PrintSettingQuery can be used to search a LibraryLocation for a set of PrintSetting objects matching the required properties.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: execute
methods.description: Query for specific PrintSettings. This PrintSettingQuery query.

properties.name: filamentDiameter
properties.description: **RETIRED** The filament diameter specifies filament diameter for FFF Printer. This should match the FFF PrintSetting filament diameter in cm.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: layerHeight
properties.description: The layer height specifies layer height of the PrintSetting. This should match the PrintSetting layer height in cm.

properties.name: location
properties.description: The location specifies the location to search in the PrintSetting library. Setting the location clears any previous specified URL.

properties.name: machine
properties.description: The machine specifies which machine the found print setting are compatible with.

properties.name: material
properties.description: The case-insensitive material specifies material of the MPBF PrintSetting.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The case-insensitive name specifies the name of the PrintSetting.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: technology
properties.description: The case-insensitive technology specifies technology of the PrintSetting.

properties.name: url
properties.description: The URL specifies the location and folder to search for in the PrintSetting library. Setting the URL updates the location.

properties.name: vendor
properties.description: The case-insensitive vendor specifies vendor of the PrintSetting. Generic FFF PrintSetting doesnt have a vendor.

classType.title: PrintSettingQuery.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

execute.title: PrintSettingQuery.execute Method
execute.description: Query for specific PrintSettings. This PrintSettingQuery query.
execute.return_description: Returns a list of PrintSetting. Each returned PrintSetting matches this query.

filamentDiameter.title: PrintSettingQuery.filamentDiameter Property
filamentDiameter.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. The filament diameter specifies filament diameter for FFF Printer. This should match the FFF PrintSetting filament diameter in cm. Remarks

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = printSettingQuery_var.filamentDiameter

# Set the value of the property.
printSettingQuery_var.filamentDiameter = propertyValue

isValid.title: PrintSettingQuery.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = printSettingQuery_var.isValid
